narrative:
The Zurich Zebras enter today’s slate with significant firepower at the top and middle of the order. Yordan Alvarez anchors the lineup with a projected 117.14, benefiting from a massive 30% SP skill advantage and a strong favorable platoon split against Kochanowicz. Pete Crow-Armstrong is our secondary headliner; despite a modest base, his elite BvP history and the 23% SP skill bump against Jake Irvin position him for a high-ceiling performance. Trea Turner rounds out the core, utilizing a significant speed advantage against a slow-catching battery to capitalize on his elite leadoff spot and favorable platoon matchup against MacKenzie Gore.

We are monitoring potential drag from Carson Benge and Caleb Durbin, whose sub-50 projections reflect difficult SP skill matchups that necessitate disciplined plate approaches to maintain Roto efficiency. While Schwarber’s projected output is buoyed by his xwOBA profile, the L/L platoon disadvantage creates a volatile floor that bears watching as game-time temperatures evolve. With our core hitters boasting strong BvP credentials—particularly Crow-Armstrong and Turner—we are well-positioned to chase counting stats, provided we minimize the drag from the lower third of the order.
